title: Cross-Encoder, Late-Interaction, Diversity, and Reranking · Behavior Case: Rag Reranker Budget Fallback
summary: A cross-encoder scores the whole corpus online, reranks only five dense candidates, ignores latency and diversity, and is declared better from one question. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reranker budget fallback increment in budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Set candidate, token, batch, time, memory, retry, and fallback budgets and test quality under timeout and partial failure.
Balance relevance, novelty, source diversity, conflict coverage, and duplicate suppression without hiding minority evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-reranker-budget-fallback — Set candidate, token, batch, time, memory, retry, and fallback budgets and test quality under timeout and partial failure.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-reranking-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-reranking-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_reranking_5(candidate_count: int = 40, rerank_count: int = 12, accepted_count: int = 5, cross_encoder_ms: float = 36.0, late_interaction: bool = False, evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if candidate_count <= 0 or rerank_count <= 0:
        return False, 0, "candidate-budget"
    if rerank_count > candidate_count or accepted_count > rerank_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "rerank-shape"
    if cross_encoder_ms <= 0.0:
        return False, accepted_count, "latency-evidence"
    if late_interaction and rerank_count < accepted_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "late-interaction-state"
    if not evaluated:
        return False, accepted_count, "quality-gate"
    assert 0 < accepted_count <= rerank_count
    return True, accepted_count, "reranked-context"
